title: Number of 1 Bits
hints: Count the number of set bits (1s) in the binary representation of A.
You can check each bit position from 0 to 31 using bitwise AND: A & (1 << i).
If the result is non-zero, increment the count.
Alternatively, you can use Brian Kernighan's algorithm: repeatedly clear the rightmost set bit using A = A & (A - 1) until A becomes 0.
Another approach: use built-in functions like Integer.bitCount() in Java, but try implementing it manually first.
